description: A round shape where all points are equal distance from center; a group arranged in this form. 'Sorui' names the shape of equality—where no one sits at the head.
sound_symbolism: The word curves complete. 'So' sweeps with enclosing motion, circle forming. 'Ru' runs around the perimeter, form continuing. Then 'i' rises to completion, the circle closed.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'lo miona sorui norelu.' (The people form a circle.) The shape of 'lona' (meeting) and 'kowela' (council).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Circle as wholeness—the mandala form, complete and containing.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Circle as equality—the ancient form where all can see each other, none elevated.
